title: Interpret System Faults Versus AGV System Faults On the Overall System RMS Page
summary: Use the Overall System RMS page layout to determine whether a displayed fault is presented as a general system fault or an AGV system fault. The source states that system faults appear on the left side of the page and active AGV faults appear on the right side, with example interpretations for communication loss and an AGV commanded to move but not moving.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing the Overall System RMS page to categorize a displayed fault by where it appears on the page, or when explaining the source-backed distinction between system faults and AGV system faults.
not_for: Do not use to infer fault meanings beyond the source-provided page layout and examples.
Do not use if the Overall System RMS page is unavailable or blank and the fault areas cannot be viewed.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the Overall System RMS page and classify displayed faults using the source-defined left-versus-right layout.

safety_notes: The source indicates the RMS page may appear blank if firewall access is not open.
Do not infer additional fault meanings beyond the source-provided examples.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Overall System RMS page or a source-backed view of it
Source-backed understanding of the left-versus-right fault layout

access_required: Access to the Overall System RMS page or a source-backed view of it
system_preconditions: The Overall System RMS page is available and visible enough to review the left and right fault areas.

visual_references.description: Training slide/frame showing the Overall System RMS page layout and noting that system faults appear on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

steps.title: View the Overall System RMS page
steps.purpose: Establish the page needed to interpret fault placement.
steps.instruction: Open or view the Overall System RMS page if it is available.
steps.expected_result: The Overall System RMS page or a source-backed image of it is visible for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The page layout can be seen well enough to identify the left and right fault areas.
steps.failure_condition: The page is unavailable, blank, or not visible enough to review the fault areas.

actions.label: View Overall System RMS page
actions.exact_action: Open or view the Overall System RMS page if it is available.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall System RMS page layout showing that system faults are on the left and active AGV faults are on the right; note that the page may appear blank if firewall access is blocked.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the RMS page is unavailable or blank and the fault areas cannot be viewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Overall System RMS, this page will show the Geek+ RMS web page, will also show system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

steps.title: Check the left side for system faults
steps.purpose: Identify whether the displayed issue is categorized by the source as a system fault.
steps.instruction: Check the left side of the Overall System RMS page for system faults.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the displayed fault appears in the system faults area on the left.
steps.healthy_condition: The fault is clearly shown on the left when it is being presented as a system fault.
steps.failure_condition: The left-side fault area cannot be reviewed or the displayed issue is not visible there when expected.

actions.label: Inspect left-side fault area
actions.exact_action: Check the left side of the page for system faults.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Left side of the Overall System RMS page where the source says system faults are shown.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the left-side fault area cannot be viewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: On the left, you'll see system faults and on the right there's AGV system faults.

steps.title: Check the right side for active AGV faults
steps.purpose: Identify whether the displayed issue is categorized by the source as an AGV system fault.
steps.instruction: Check the right side of the Overall System RMS page for active AGV faults.
steps.expected_result: You determine whether the displayed fault appears in the active AGV faults area on the right.
steps.healthy_condition: The fault is clearly shown on the right when it is being presented as an AGV system fault.
steps.failure_condition: The right-side fault area cannot be reviewed or the displayed issue is not visible there when expected.

actions.label: Inspect right-side fault area
actions.exact_action: Check the right side of the page for active AGV faults.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Right side of the Overall System RMS page where the source says active AGV faults are shown.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the right-side fault area cannot be viewed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: On the left, you'll see system faults and on the right there's AGV system faults.
This page will show the Geek+ RMS web page, will also show system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

steps.title: Interpret communication loss as a left-side system fault example
steps.purpose: Apply the source-provided example for communication loss to the page layout.
steps.instruction: If a communication loss with one of the carts is being discussed or displayed, interpret that as a system fault shown on the left based on the source explanation.
steps.expected_result: Communication-loss issues are categorized as left-side system faults when using this source.
steps.healthy_condition: A communication-loss issue is interpreted as belonging to the left-side system fault area.
steps.failure_condition: A communication-loss issue is interpreted outside the source-backed left-side system fault area.

actions.label: Apply communication-loss example
actions.exact_action: If a communication loss with one of the carts is being discussed or displayed, interpret that as a system fault shown on the left based on the source explanation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the page layout while applying the source example that communication loss appears as a system fault on the left.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the issue requires interpretation beyond the source-provided examples.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If we lose communication with one of the chats, it'll be on the left.

steps.title: Interpret AGV told to move but not moving as a right-side AGV fault example
steps.purpose: Apply the source-provided example for an AGV move issue to the page layout.
steps.instruction: If an AGV was told to move but has not gone in progress, interpret that as an AGV system fault shown on the right based on the source explanation.
steps.expected_result: An AGV commanded to move but not moving is categorized as a right-side AGV system fault when using this source.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV move issue is interpreted as belonging to the right-side AGV system fault area.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV move issue is interpreted outside the source-backed right-side AGV system fault area.

actions.label: Apply AGV move example
actions.exact_action: If an AGV was told to move but has not gone in progress, interpret that as an AGV system fault shown on the right based on the source explanation.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the page layout while applying the source example that an AGV told to move but not moving appears on the right.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the issue requires interpretation beyond the source-provided examples.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If we told an AGV to move but it hasn't moved, it'll be on the right.
On the left, you'll see system faults and on the right there's AGV system faults.

steps.title: Record which side contains the fault
steps.purpose: Document the observed category using the source-backed page layout.
steps.instruction: Record which side of the page contains the fault so the observed issue is categorized using the source-backed page layout.
steps.expected_result: The issue is documented as a left-side system fault or right-side AGV system fault based on what was observed.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded category matches the side of the page where the fault appears.
steps.failure_condition: The issue is recorded without confirming which side of the page contains the fault, or the record adds unsupported meaning beyond the source.

actions.label: Document fault side
actions.exact_action: Record which side of the page contains the fault so the observed issue is categorized using the source-backed page layout.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if categorization would require unsupported interpretation beyond left-side system fault versus right-side AGV system fault.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: On the left, you'll see system faults and on the right there's AGV system faults.

success_criteria: The user can distinguish whether the displayed issue is being presented as a system fault or an AGV system fault on the RMS page.
The observed fault is categorized according to the source-backed left-versus-right page layout.
healthy_conditions: The Overall System RMS page is visible.
System faults are reviewed on the left side of the page.
Active AGV faults are reviewed on the right side of the page.
Communication-loss examples are interpreted as left-side system faults.
AGV told-to-move-but-not-moving examples are interpreted as right-side AGV system faults.
failure_conditions: The Overall System RMS page is unavailable or blank and the fault areas cannot be viewed.
The left or right fault areas cannot be reviewed.
The user infers meanings beyond the source-provided examples and page layout.
The issue cannot be categorized from the visible page layout.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the RMS page is unavailable or blank and the fault areas cannot be viewed.
Escalate if the page layout cannot be confirmed from the available source-backed view.
Do not extend interpretation beyond the source-provided examples without additional validated guidance.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Overall System RMS page layout showing system faults on the left and active AGV faults on the right.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: On the left, you'll see system faults and on the right there's AGV system faults. If we lose communication with one of the chats, it'll be on the left. If we told an AGV to move but it hasn't moved, it'll be on the right.

missing_details: The source does not provide a navigation path for opening the Overall System RMS page.
The source does not provide a formal documentation location or recording method for the categorized fault.
The source does not define additional fault examples beyond communication loss and an AGV told to move but not moving.
The source does not provide timing estimates for this reference procedure.
